perturbation.original_text: B. During the Initial Term, iVillage will design, create and deliver [*] Ford-branded advertising units. The advertising units shall be subject to FMM's final approval. iVillage will deliver approximately [*] new advertising units each during the Promotion Period. For purposes of this Agreement, an advertising unit can include but shall not be limited to banners in the form of rich media, java-based, animated, daughter and/or pull-down banners, or a combination of appropriate technologies, and which shall represent and be defined by industry standards.
perturbation.changed_text: B. During the Initial Term, iVillage will design, create and deliver [*] Ford-branded advertising units. The advertising units shall be subject to FMM's final approval. iVillage will deliver approximately [*] new advertising units each during the Promotion Period. For purposes of this Agreement, an advertising unit will be determined at iVillage's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The last sentence of section 2.B. originally gave a clear (though not exhaustive) definition of 'advertising unit'. Removing it introduces ambiguity as the definition is now at the sole discretion of iVillage, potentially contradicting Section 9 (Licenses) which requires iVillage to obtain licenses for Ford's trademarks, service marks and logos, and which could be undermined if iVillage chose to define 'advertising units' in a manner that does not align with typical understanding or licensing requirements, potentially leading to legal disputes over brand representation and intellectual property usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FMM grants to iVillage, during the Initial Term of this Agreement, a royalty-free, non-exclusive, worldwide license to use, reproduce and display Ford's tradenames, trademarks, service marks and logos (collectively, the "Marks") in connection with this Agreement. No right, title, license, or interest in any Marks owned by Ford or any of its affiliates is intended to be given to or acquired by iVillage by the execution of or the performance of this Agreement. iVillage shall not use the Marks for any purpose or activity except as expressly authorized or contemplated herein;
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants iVillage sole discretion in defining 'advertising unit,' potentially allowing them to define it in a way that infringes on Ford's trademarks, service marks, and logos, contradicting the provision that requires iVillage to obtain licenses for using the 'Marks' and prohibits them from using the Marks for any purpose not expressly authorized. The original text gave some parameters, but the new text has none.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Network or the Bridge Site are unavailable for a substantial period of time due an event of force majeur or otherwise, iVillage agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to "make good" any impressions lost as a result of such circumstance.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Network or the Bridge Site are unavailable for a substantial period of time due an event of force majeur or otherwise, iVillage has no obligation to use commercially reasonable efforts to "make good" any impressions lost as a result of such circumstance.
perturbation.explanation: By explicitly stating that iVillage has no obligation to 'make good' lost impressions due to force majeure, while Section 2.C.(iii) states that iVillage needs to either extend Initial Term of the agreement to make good the undelivered impressions, or refund to FMM an amount equal to [*] for each [*] impressions which were not delivered, this creates a direct contradiction. The amendment removes an obligation stated here, while there's still a need to make good on undelivered impressions somewhere else in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (iii) In the event that iVillage fails to deliver the advertising impressions during the Promotion Period, FMM shall have the option of either (a) extending the Initial Term of this Agreement for an additional three (3) month period to "make good" the undelivered impressions or (b) requiring iVillage to refund to FMM an amount equal to [*] for each [*] impressions which were not delivered.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly removes iVillage's obligation to 'make good' lost impressions, while the contradicted text (iii) provides FMM the option to extend the agreement or receive a refund for undelivered impressions. This creates a direct conflict as the changed text eliminates the need to compensate for undelivered impressions, contradicting the contradicted text's provision for making good those impressions through extension or refund. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: FMM and FMC's respective advertising agencies shall be free to provide input with respect to the aforementioned promotional efforts set forth in this section and shall have the opportunity to reasonably approve such efforts.
perturbation.changed_text: FMM and FMC's respective advertising agencies shall be free to provide input with respect to the aforementioned promotional efforts set forth in this section.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'and shall have the opportunity to reasonably approve such efforts' from section 2.D creates a contradiction in enforceability. Section 2A states, 'Upon receipt from iVillage of the proposed Bridge Site design and content, FMM shall have no more than five (5) business days in which to provide iVillage with its acceptance or rejection of the design and content. If iVillage does not receive FMM's acceptance or rejection of such within the allotted time, iVillage shall deem FMM's silence as acceptance.' Section 2A gives FMM right of acceptance and rejection, but now, Section 2D takes that away, giving just the opportunity to give input.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Production Period, iVillage will design, develop, construct and host a Ford bridge site (the "Bridge Site") which shall include approximately [*] pages of content and other interactive material such as a travel planner or a car design feature. During the Promotion Period, iVillage will continue to host, maintain and update the Bridge Site. Upon receipt from iVillage of the proposed Bridge Site design and content, FMM shall have no more than five (5) business days in which to provide iVillage with its acceptance or rejection of the design and content. If iVillage does not receive FMM's acceptance or rejection of such within the allotted time, iVillage shall deem FMM's silence as acceptance. The Parties shall work together to determine the content mix and delivery deadlines in order to maximize the effectiveness of the sponsorship campaign.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.D removes the advertising agencies' right to 'reasonably approve such efforts', while Section 2A explicitly grants FMM the right to accept or reject the Bridge Site design and content. This creates a direct conflict: Section 2A gives FMM ultimate approval power, but the modified Section 2.D only allows for input from advertising agencies, diminishing the approval rights granted in 2A, thus causing an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.
